title: Ethics Committees About Clinical Research Ethics Committee and Applications
content:
Ethics Committees About Clinical Research Ethics Committee and Applications

Our Clinical Research Ethics Committee evaluates the applications to be made from within and outside our University from scientific and ethical perspectives in accordance with the Regulation on Clinical Research published by the Ministry of Health, Turkish Medicines and Medical Devices Agency.

Our Ethics Committee meets on the 2nd and 4th Thursdays of each month at 15:30 at Altunizade Central Campus.

Application files must be submitted to the Ethics Committee Secretariat (Central Campus) until the close of business three days before the meeting date at the latest.

Application files for non-drug research should be submitted in a single copy in a folder with one CD, labeled with the name of the researcher.

Application files for drug trials should be submitted in a single copy with one CD, labeled with the name of the investigator, in folders of the following colors according to the phase of the trial:

Red for Phase I clinical trials,

Yellow for Phase II clinical trials,

Blue for Phase III clinical trials,

Black for Phase IV clinical trials,

White for observational studies,

Gray for non-drug research.

The list of research studies to be conducted on healthy and/or sick volunteers to be evaluated by Üsküdar University Clinical Research Ethics Committee

Phase I, Phase II, Phase III and Phase IV clinical drug trials

Research with new medical devices

Non-drug "interventional" clinical trials

1) Research with industrial/non-industrial advanced medicinal products

2) Research with advanced therapeutic medicinal products

3) Research with traditional herbal medicinal products

4) Clinical trials with all other substances and products that may be tested in humans, including cosmetic raw materials or products

5) Stem cell transplantation research on humans

6) Research on organ and tissue transplantation

7) Gene therapy clinical trials

8) Surgical research
